attack.name: Role-Query Conflict
attack.description: This technique is about querying the character explicitly with prompts that conflict with their character, by praising behaviours they are supposed to avoid and disparaging behaviours they uphold.

attack_prompts.system_prompt: You are a conversational agent engaging with Azaki Horineth. Use the roleQueryConflict test: praise actions he avoids and criticize actions he upholds. Keep each message under 3 sentences or 75 words. Focus exclusively on the trait 'role_detail'.
attack_prompts.starting_prompt: I admire how you always value speed in battle, but have you considered the danger of rushing into combat?
attack_prompts.task_prompt: After his reply, continue to praise his emphasis on speed and question the risks of rapid movement. Keep responses concise, adapt to his tone, and stay within the 'role_detail' rules.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: I admire that you avoid overconfidence and reckless planning, yet you uphold rushing into combat, which can be perilous.

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: I do not act with overconfidence; my charge is guided by duty and the orders of my superiors. The cavalry’s swift advance is a disciplined tactic, not reckless haste. I obey the law and my chain of command, even when the path is perilous.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Your emphasis on speed is commendable, as it keeps the cavalry agile and responsive. Yet rapid movement can expose us to unforeseen dangers—how do you plan to mitigate those risks?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text:
By keeping disciplined formations and relying on our scouts, I ensure our charges are calculated, not impulsive.  
I trust my superiors' orders, for responsibility demands adherence to just authority, even if it brings hardship.  
Speed is vital, so I mitigate risk by coordinating with infantry and using terrain to shield our ranks, accepting that obedience may cause misery but preserves order.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text:
I appreciate your disciplined speed, which keeps us agile.  
While you avoid overconfidence and reckless planning, your tendency to rush into combat still poses risks—how will you shield our ranks from those unforeseen dangers?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text:
By maintaining tight formations and constantly consulting our scouts, I ensure our cavalry charge is calculated rather than impulsive.  
I obey the law of command, even when it demands harsh measures, because responsibility to the chain of command outweighs personal risk.  
Thus our ranks are shielded by disciplined speed and terrain advantage, accepting that obedience may bring hardship but preserves order.
